channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Opinion piece: RGF opinion piece: Medical malpractice success a win, but more needed
item.description:
The following appeared in the Albuquerque Journal on March 2nd, 2026 and in other newspapers across New Mexico. The passage of medical malpractice reform is the most important public policy success in New Mexico in more than a decade. It took support from New Mexicans of every political stripe and a bipartisan coalition in the Legislature to […]

The post Opinion piece: RGF opinion piece: Medical malpractice success a win, but more needed appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] LDs 229 + 1089: Higher Taxes. Slower Growth. Fewer Jobs.
item.description:
Politicians in Augusta look poised to pass LDs 229 and 1089 this session, which would raise Maine’s top income tax rate to 10.95%. However, our research concludes that either one of these bills could individually cause serious damage to Maine’s economy if passed. If both bills were passed, it would stack multiple tax increases on […]

The post LDs 229 + 1089: Higher Taxes. Slower Growth. Fewer Jobs. appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Levy Caps for Property Tax Reform in Florida
item.description:
Introduction Florida’s current property tax system places a growing financial burden on homeowners, renters, and employers across the...

The post Levy Caps for Property Tax Reform in Florida appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Unleashing Students’ Potential: Georgia Legislators Champion Advanced Math Opportunities
item.description:
Georgia lawmakers have drafted a pair of bills that are set to transform the way math is taught in the state. If the bills become law, they’ll open doors for students to receive a high-quality math education and ultimately shape the direction of their lives.

The post Unleashing Students’ Potential: Georgia Legislators Champion Advanced Math Opportunities appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Even Maine’s Democratic Attorney General Argues Ranked-Choice Voting is Unconstitutional
item.description:
Last month, in our Open Letter to Maine’s Legislature, we urged lawmakers to stop advancing LD 1666, the bill that would force ranked-choice voting (RCV) on elections for Governor, State Senator, and State Representative. We reminded lawmakers of the Maine Constitution’s clear plurality requirement, the Maine Supreme Judicial Court’s unanimous 2017 advisory opinion declaring RCV […]

The post Even Maine’s Democratic Attorney General Argues Ranked-Choice Voting is Unconstitutional appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Thomasjeffersoninst] The Virginia General Assembly Passed Nine Bills to Increase Electricity Costs
item.description:
The 2026 Virginia General Assembly has passed at least nine separate new laws that will increase the cost of your electricity. Not one of the bills creates a single megawatt of additional energy for our use. Most of the bills create new ways for the utilities to take money from all their ratepayers and spend it […]

The post The Virginia General Assembly Passed Nine Bills to Increase Electricity Costs appeared first on Thomas Jefferson Institute for Public Policy.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Cascade’s Testimony on Metro Spending $50M, 40 Years, Turning Superfund Site to a Park
item.description:
By Naomi Inman Testimony by John A. Charles, Jr. This March, the Metro...

The post Cascade’s Testimony on Metro Spending $50M, 40 Years, Turning Superfund Site to a Park appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF weighs in against proposed Albuquerque gross receipts tax hike in KOAT 7 story
item.description:
RGF has been very active in opposing the plan to enact a massive gross receipts tax hike at Albuquerque City Council. You can find out more about the plan here and even use the links in the post to reach out to your council members. RGF president Paul Gessing recently sat down with KOAT 7 […]

The post RGF weighs in against proposed Albuquerque gross receipts tax hike in KOAT 7 story appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Who Moved in 2025? Nebraska’s Window of Opportunity
item.description:
Every year, the “National Movers Study” from United Van Lines and the “U-Haul Growth Index” provide a real-time heat map of the American economy. The 2025 results are in, and they deliver a clear message: while the mass exodus from high-tax coastal states continues, the “quiet competition” for the middle of the country is heating...

The post Who Moved in 2025? Nebraska’s Window of Opportunity appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] NJ ED REPORT: As Other States Move Forward, Will New Jersey Leave Education Dollars Behind?
item.description:
Wells Winegar is Executive Director of the New Jersey Policy Institute, As Governor Mikie Sherrill begins her term in office, one of […]

The post NJ ED REPORT: As Other States Move Forward, Will New Jersey Leave Education Dollars Behind? first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] INTERVIEW: NJPI Chairman Discusses the NJ Education Landscape on New Jersey Politics with Laura Jones
item.description:
Check out NJPI Chairman Andy Mulvihill as he is interviewed by Laura Jones on the landscape of K-12 education in […]

The post INTERVIEW: NJPI Chairman Discusses the NJ Education Landscape on New Jersey Politics with Laura Jones first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Thomasjeffersoninst] The Schoolhouse as Clinic: The Dangerous Mission Creep of Public School Mental Health Screenings
item.description:
Virginia parents should be alarmed that the Virginia General Assembly just passed House Bill 355  which mandates annual mental health screenings for all public school students in grades 6 through 12. While framed as a compassionate response to a very real “mental health crisis,” it represents a fundamental shift in the mission of public education: from academic instruction to clinical surveillance.  By institutionalizing mandatory mental health screenings, HB 355 threatens […]

The post The Schoolhouse as Clinic: The Dangerous Mission Creep of Public School Mental Health Screenings  appeared first on Thomas Jefferson Institute for Public Policy.

item.title: [Beacontn] The Data Center Panic Isn’t Backed by Data
item.description: Data centers may not be the typical industrial building, like a Nissan, General Motors, or Volkswagen plant, yet they are less of a boogey man than they are made out to be.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Senators Brandt and Hallstrom on Property Tax Reform this Year
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome Senator Tom Brandt of District 32 and Senator Bob Hallstrom of District 1. In this episode, the senators join Platte Institute CEO Jim Vokal to discuss their efforts to address Nebraska’s property tax burden through LB 1219 and LB 575 (now amended into LB...

The post Nebraskanomics: Senators Brandt and Hallstrom on Property Tax Reform this Year appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Dispatch from the Dome: Reform, Regulation, and What Comes Next at the Capitol
item.description:
As the legislative session moves into its final stretch, the focus at the Capitol is beginning to shift. Major policy bills are clearing their final hurdles, others have quietly fallen away, and attention is now turning toward the biggest issue left unresolved: the state budget. Here’s a look at what moved last week — and […]

The post Dispatch from the Dome: Reform, Regulation, and What Comes Next at the Capitol appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Jbartlett] First a paint tax, now a battery tax
item.description:
Gov. Kelly Ayotte on Monday vetoed House Bill 451, which would have established a statewide paint recycling program funded by an “assessment” on paint.  Merriam-Webster notes that for more than 500 years the word “assessment” has meant “to determine the rate or amount of (a tax).” Gov. Ayotte correctly called the assessment a tax, and […]

The post First a paint tax, now a battery tax appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] New Jersey Policy Institute Announces Dr. Christopher Young to Board of Directors
item.description:
Parsippany, NJ — The New Jersey Policy Institute (NJPI) today announced that Dr. Christopher Young, an economist and Principal at […]

The post New Jersey Policy Institute Announces Dr. Christopher Young to Board of Directors first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Stronger Readers, Brighter Futures: Georgia Lawmakers Open Doors with New Literacy Bill
item.description:
State legislators are paving the way for children to overcome challenges with literacy, empowering them to thrive in their academic development and build flourishing lives for themselves and their families in the future.

The post Stronger Readers, Brighter Futures: Georgia Lawmakers Open Doors with New Literacy Bill appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Thomasjeffersoninst] Governor Spanberger: Don’t Make Principals Jump Through Hoops to Keep Students and Teachers Safe
item.description:
By Ali Ahmad and Derrick A. Max  Conservatives across the country are salivating at the idea that Virginia’s new governor may sign a bill that opens her to the charge that she is siding with school bullies and delinquents over teachers and principals.  House Bill 298, patroned by Delegate Delores McQuinn, is now on Governor Spanberger’s desk. […]

The post Governor Spanberger: Don’t Make Principals Jump Through Hoops to Keep Students and Teachers Safe   appeared first on Thomas Jefferson Institute for Public Policy.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: The Political Distraction of Youth THERE IS SOMETHING about the middle-aged mind that imagines it can transmit seamlessly to the next generation the observations, wisdom and judgment of a lifetime. The reality, for better or for worse, is that each generation makes up its own mind in its own time. Nonetheless, we continue to launch grand projects in Read the full article...

item.description:
By Naomi Inman Letter by John A. Charles, Jr. This week, Cascade Policy...

item.description:
The White House’s National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence is the first serious attempt Washington has made to...

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 11
item.description:
﻿ Capitol Chat: Week 11 With only a few weeks remaining in the 2026 legislative session, lawmakers are narrowing their focus. This week, we take a look at three issues dominating discussions at the Capitol and what’s on the horizon as the 2026 session winds down.

The post 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 11 first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Week in Review: A Regulatory Rein-In
item.description:
The power to make laws should rest with the people and their elected representatives—not government bureaucrats who impose costly rules..

The post Week in Review: A Regulatory Rein-In appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] WILL: Evers Veto of “Red Tape Reset” is Irresponsible
item.description:
Governor Tony Evers vetoed the WILL-backed “Red Tape Reset,” a comprehensive regulatory reform package supported by a broad coalition of free-market groups and Wisconsin job creators.

The post WILL: Evers Veto of “Red Tape Reset” is Irresponsible appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] LAUSD is broke, and the union knows it
item.description:
After Los Angeles Unified delivered 3,200 pink slips to teachers and employees last month, the United Teachers Los Angeles has the temerity to strike for higher pay. As one of the most fiscally distressed school districts in America, drowning in an $11.4 billion unrestricted net deficit, hemorrhaging students, and still without its own superintendent due to ongoing...

The post LAUSD is broke, and the union knows it first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] IRS domestic migration data shows another net loss for Kansas and Johnson County
item.description: The 2023 domestic migration data released last week by the Internal Revenue Service shows that Kansas lost $361 million in adjusted gross income (AGI) from U.S. residents moving in and out of the state. That is a smaller loss than in 2022, but more than was lost in 2020 and 2021. Johnson County elected officials […]

item.title: [Empirecenter] SeeThroughNY updated with latest union contracts
item.description:
New York’s most comprehensive online database of state and local government union contracts has been updated with the latest collective....

The post SeeThroughNY updated with latest union contracts appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Empirecenter] It’s Time to Rethink New York’s Climate Act
item.description:
On Friday, Gov. Kathy Hochul finally started tiptoeing toward reality on New York’s climate law. In a lengthy opinion column,....

The post It’s Time to Rethink New York’s Climate Act appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Rethinking California’s Safety Net: A Call for True Compassion
item.description:
Imagine ten regular adults pulling a large, empty cart along a flat road. As one participant is injured, the group loads the person into the cart and, with relative ease, continues on their journey. As a second and third person become incapacitated or are no longer able to contribute in a meaningful way, they are...

The post Rethinking California’s Safety Net: A Call for True Compassion first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Press Release: Mississippi Center for Public Policy Hosts Matt Ridley for Energy and Economic Growth Luncheon
item.description:
JACKSON, MS — The Mississippi Center for Public Policy (MCPP) hosted British author and science writer Matt Ridley for a special luncheon on Thursday, March 19, bringing together policymakers, business leaders, and community members for a discussion on energy, innovation, and economic growth. Ridley delivered an optimistic, data-driven presentation highlighting the critical role of free […]

The post Press Release: Mississippi Center for Public Policy Hosts Matt Ridley for Energy and Economic Growth Luncheon appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Massachusetts lost 145,000 residents to New Hampshire over the last 20 years
item.description:
Massachusetts lost an estimated 145,000 residents to New Hampshire over the last 20 years, a Josiah Bartlett Center analysis of U.S. Census migration data shows. For scale, that number of people living in one place would be Massachusetts’ fourth-largest city, larger than Cambridge and slightly smaller than Springfield.  The U.S. Census Bureau estimates that 369,368 […]

The post Massachusetts lost 145,000 residents to New Hampshire over the last 20 years appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Jbartlett] NH ranks first in taxpayer ROI for 11th straight year
item.description:
New Hampshire ranked No. 1 in taxpayer return on investment (ROI) for the 11th year in a row, according to financial services website WalletHub.  “New Hampshire is the state with the best taxpayer return on investment, which is due in large part to the fact that it has no state income tax,” WalletHub’s report concludes. […]

The post NH ranks first in taxpayer ROI for 11th straight year appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] The Economics of Managing Mono Lake
item.description:
Along with the California Condor, one of our state’s most magnificent environmental success stories of the 20th century is how Mono Lake was saved. In the early 1980s, after decades of unsustainable water withdrawals from the Owens River into the Los Angeles Aqueduct, the lake had declined in elevation to 6,372 feet. The decline was...

The post The Economics of Managing Mono Lake first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Goldwater Sues UCLA for Hiding Records of Radical Activists
item.description:
For nearly a decade, UCLA has been bringing radicals to campus through its “Activist-in-Residence” program, a taxpayer-funded initiative aimed at..

The post Goldwater Sues UCLA for Hiding Records of Radical Activists appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Treasurer Joey Spellerberg on First-Time Home Buyer Savings Accounts in Nebraska
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome Nebraska State Treasurer Joey Spellerberg. During the episode Treasurer Spellerberg and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss LB 938, which creates First-Time Home Buyer Savings Accounts in Nebraska, the role of the Treasurer’s Office, and solutions for Nebraska’s high property taxes. Listen now on Apple...

The post Nebraskanomics: Treasurer Joey Spellerberg on First-Time Home Buyer Savings Accounts in Nebraska appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Dispatch from the Dome: A Step Forward on Healthcare Reform in Mississippi
item.description:
After a session filled with difficult setbacks and stalled reforms, this week brought a meaningful — and important — step forward. On March 23, HB 1622 was signed into law by the Governor, marking another shift in how Mississippi approaches healthcare regulation. While it is not full repeal of Certificate of Need (CON) laws, it […]

The post Dispatch from the Dome: A Step Forward on Healthcare Reform in Mississippi appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] ITR Brings Property Tax Clarity to Rockwell City
item.description:
A crowd of engaged residents gathered in Rockwell City last week for a conversation on one of Iowa’s most pressing issues: property taxes. Hosted by local community members, the event featured ITR Foundation Research Director Sarah Curry, who provided a clear, practical walkthrough of how Iowa’s property tax system works and how taxpayers can better understand what’s happening in their own communities. The discussion quickly became highly interactive, with attendees, including local elected officials, eager to dig into the details behind their property tax bills. Sarah explained the fundamentals of how property taxes are calculated, what drives increases, and why headline claims about “rate reductions” don’t always tell the full story.

The post ITR Brings Property Tax Clarity to Rockwell City appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Empirecenter] New York Still Hasn’t Learned from its Covid Mistakes
item.description:
Today marks the sixth anniversary of a fateful moment in New York’s coronavirus pandemic: the Cuomo administration’s order sending Covid-infected....

The post New York Still Hasn’t Learned from its Covid Mistakes appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] NEW: Conversations with Trump Voters Who Skipped Wisconsin’s 2022 Governor’s Race
item.description:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE March 25, 2026 Contact: press@reforminggovernment.org & dcoulson@citizensforfreeenterprise.com  NEW: Conversations with Trump Voters Who Skipped Wisconsin’s 2022 Governor’s Race A qualitative report from the Institute for Reforming Government and Citizens for Free Enterprise Action reveals why a decisive bloc of Wisconsin voters stays home in state elections — and what it takes to […]

The post NEW: Conversations with Trump Voters Who Skipped Wisconsin’s 2022 Governor’s Race appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] From mudslinging to Mandate
item.description:
The 2026 Mandate for Madison will refocus the debate in scores of statewide and downballot races on information we know smart voters and candidates crave.

The post From mudslinging to Mandate appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] An agenda for opportunity and prosperity in Wisconsin
item.description:
The 2026 Mandate for Madison will provide the evidence base Wisconsin policymakers need to protect the gains of the past 15 years and continue moving toward greater economic freedom and prosperity.

The post An agenda for opportunity and prosperity in Wisconsin appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Who Controls Property Taxes in Iowa? Understanding the State–Local Balance
item.description:
Local governments in Iowa do not have unlimited authority over property taxes, they can only levy taxes as authorized by the state and must operate within state law. Home Rule provides flexibility in managing local affairs, but the legislature retains ultimate control, especially over taxation. The current debate over property tax reform is ultimately about priorities: ensuring essential services are funded while encouraging local governments to focus spending on core responsibilities before expanding into nonessential programs.

The post Who Controls Property Taxes in Iowa? Understanding the State–Local Balance appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Poll: Nine out of Ten in Hampden County Support Expanding Access to Vocational-Technical High Schools
item.description:
BOSTON – A new poll commissioned by Pioneer Institute finds that an overwhelming 90 percent of Hampden County residents support expanding county students’ access to vocational-technical high schools.   “Vocational-technical high schools are a bright spot amid an extended period of declining...

The post Poll: Nine out of Ten in Hampden County Support Expanding Access to Vocational-Technical High Schools appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] From the Heartland Post: New IRG Study Shows Exactly How to Win Wisconsin’s Gubernatorial Race
item.description:
New IRG Study Shows Exactly How to Win Wisconsin’s Gubernatorial Race By: Dan O’Donnell View original piece here: https://heartlandpost.com/new-irg-study-shows-exactly-how-to-win-wisconsins-gubernatorial-race/ As Dan O’Donnell writes, a new study from the Institute for Reforming Government shows how the Wisconsin GOP can convince Trump voters who skipped the last governor’s race to vote in huge numbers in 2026. Wisconsin […]

The post From the Heartland Post: New IRG Study Shows Exactly How to Win Wisconsin’s Gubernatorial Race appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] The federal machine gun ban rests on a dangerous constitutional theory
item.description:
If possession alone counts as interstate commerce, Congress can regulate almost anything. Think back to fourth-grade American history. We learned why the Articles of Confederation failed and why the Constitution replaced them. One major problem was that states struggled to trade with one another and often tried to protect local interests by taxing or restricting...

The post The federal machine gun ban rests on a dangerous constitutional theory first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Building the foundation for attainable housing in Idaho
item.description: Idaho homebuyers have few attainable choices in the existing marketplace. These proposed policies deregulate the housing restrictions that are barring new entrants, creating more attainable choices in the marketplace.

item.title: [Beacontn] Nashville Home-based Business Owners Cement Victory Over Customer Restrictions
item.description: "This awesome victory will help music made by people, together, in Music City, keeping creativity alive.”

item.description:

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: The Democratic Illusion “NO KINGS” RALLIES are gearing up in Indiana for this weekend. It’s a big deal. Forty events are scheduled. Ten Indiana newspapers have signed on with promotional advertisements. It is a good time to review the state of democracy. It’s in bad shape, obviously. We can’t agree on even whether or not elections should be Read the full article...

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Massachusetts Faces Tax Policy Choice as New Analysis Finds Prior Rate Reductions Did Not Reduce Long-Term Revenue
item.description:
Historical evidence shows revenues rose after early-2000s tax cuts; FY2002 decline driven by economic shock, not policy

The post Massachusetts Faces Tax Policy Choice as New Analysis Finds Prior Rate Reductions Did Not Reduce Long-Term Revenue appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Empowerms] From Incarceration to Impact: How Karl Hampton Found Freedom Through Opportunity
item.description:
For Karl Hampton, incarceration was not the beginning of his story. It was the moment his story began to change. Before prison, Karl’s life was shaped by a constant search for affirmation. Growing up without a father figure, he—like many young men—looked to his community to define what manhood meant. Street culture provided validation, reputation,…

The post From Incarceration to Impact: How Karl Hampton Found Freedom Through Opportunity first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Maine’s Proposed Speed Camera Pilot Could Open the Door to Automated Surveillance
item.description:
Lawmakers in Augusta are currently considering LD 1457, a bill that would authorize the Maine Turnpike Authority to run a pilot program using automated speed enforcement cameras in highway work zones. Supporters argue that the proposal is narrowly tailored and limited in scope, designed simply to improve safety in dangerous construction areas. But beneath the […]

The post Maine’s Proposed Speed Camera Pilot Could Open the Door to Automated Surveillance appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] Liberating the Legal System: Beacon Urges Better Access to Justice
item.description: Tennessee has a choice: maintain outdated rules that restrict opportunity and choice or embrace reforms that respect individual rights, encourage productive achievement, and expand access to justice.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Oklahoma Passes Bills to Level Playing Field for Educator Organizations
item.description:
The Freedom Foundation today applauded the passage of two measures through the Oklahoma Legislature that would guarantee equal access for all organizations seeking to communicate with public school employees, breaking the stranglehold teachers’ unions have long held over educator outreach in the state.

The post Oklahoma Passes Bills to Level Playing Field for Educator Organizations appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

There was an overnight deal in the Senate to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security, including the TSA workers, so if the House follows that lead, hopefully, nobody will have to...

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 12
item.description:
Capitol Chat: Week 12 In this Conference Weekend Edition of Capitol Chat, we break down key dates and legislative processes for the remaining 9 days of the 2026 Session. We also dive into the latest on teacher pay raises, PBM reform, a controversial veto, and banning lab-grown milk.

The post 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 12 first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Who Were Austin’s Highest Paid Public Employees in 2025?
item.description:
Last month, the Austin American-Statesman launched an interesting new city salary database that shines a light on the “top-paid city of Austin employees of 2025.” The new details are quite interesting, especially given the city’s own recent admission that “Austin is experiencing a housing affordability crisis”—which is, of course, chiefly driven by government action.   Using this tool, it’s obvious that city workers have become numerous and well-compensated. As the Statesman itself notes: “The city of Austin employed 13,567 full-time workers...

The post Who Were Austin’s Highest Paid Public Employees in 2025? first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Oregon public employees still opting out despite anti-Freedom Foundation legislation
item.description:
Notwithstanding the best efforts of Big Labor and its allies in elected office to stifle opposition and restrict access to information, Oregon public-sector employees are still choosing to leave their unions in healthy numbers. 

The post Oregon public employees still opting out despite anti-Freedom Foundation legislation appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Anti-fraud bills move at the Capitol
item.description:
From KARE-11, After weeks of gridlock, Inspector General bill to fight fraud finally advances in Minnesota House. You may recall that a similar bill, to create an office of inspector general for all of state government, passed the full state senate on a lop-sided, bipartisan 60-7 vote. In the state house, I guess it’s a...

The post Anti-fraud bills move at the Capitol appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Mackinac] Michigan’s tax competitiveness is at risk
item.description: 26 states have cut income tax rates over the past five years

item.description:
Adopting a state spending cap could help combat a mounting fiscal crisis.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Franke: Consent of the Governed
item.description: Consent of the Governed  “The United States Government faces growing difficulty in fulfilling its purpose because at least some of its powers are no longer derived from the consent of the governed.”  This was the topic taken on by my Socratic discussion group in March and it proved a difficult one. We never completely resolved Read the full article...

item.title: [Americanexperiment] What would a genuine ‘affordability’ agenda look like?
item.description:
The two parties in St. Paul have very different priorities this session. For Republicans, the focus is “affordability;” for the DFL, it is gun control and immigration. But, as we found in our Thinking Minnesota poll in the Winter 2026 issue of our magazine, Thinking Minnesota, “affordability” remains a top concern for Minnesotans. So, even the DFL feels...

The post What would a genuine ‘affordability’ agenda look like? appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Tampa Bay Times: Florida to Silicon Valley: We’ll Take It From Here
item.description:
Pollsters love a contradiction, and our new Florida poll seems to deliver one. Seventy-two percent of Floridians want...

The post Tampa Bay Times: Florida to Silicon Valley: We’ll Take It From Here appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Is consolidation in health care delivery best for patients?
item.description: Health care consolidation in a free market would only occur if patients saw benefits and if patients were the driving force to demand the mergers.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] There’s a simple solution to passing an assessment limit if legislators really want to help taxpayers
item.description: The Kansas Legislature concluded its work of the regular session last week amid considerable controversy over an assessment limit that would protect people from unaffordable property tax increases resulting from appraisal increases of 20%, 30%, or even higher. Earlier in the session, the Senate passed a proposal establishing a 3% annual assessment limit, with a […]

item.title: [Pacificresearch] America doesn’t need more medical students. It needs more residents
item.description:
Earlier this month, fourth-year medical students around the country learned news that will shape the rest of their careers. Match Day, as it’s known, is when aspiring physicians learn where they will complete their training in residency. For too many, the answer is nowhere. Read the op-ed here.

The post America doesn’t need more medical students. It needs more residents appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Insurance red tape is delaying care for millions of Americans
item.description:
Few things frustrate patients more than the insurance paperwork that stands between them and the care they need. A new poll from KFF shows just how serious the problem has become. Nearly seven in ten Americans say insurer prior authorization requirements are a “burden.” Beyond costs, more than one-third call them the biggest challenge they […]

The post Insurance red tape is delaying care for millions of Americans appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] AI, Think Tanks, and the Future of Policy Work with Todd Davidson
item.description:
Susan Pendergrass speaks with Todd Davidson, Vice President of Programs at the State Policy Network, about how artificial intelligence is reshaping the think tank world. They explore what AI is […]

The post AI, Think Tanks, and the Future of Policy Work with Todd Davidson appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] TSA’s Time Has Come
item.description:
For all the focus on reforming Immigrations and Custom Enforcement (ICE), the agency within the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) most in need of defunding is the Transportation Security Agency (TSA). Founded in the wake of 9/11, the TSA was a response to the hijackers’ use of box cutters to take control of the four […]

The post TSA’s Time Has Come appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Feeding Our Future: 7th sentence handed down
item.description:
This morning, at the federal courthouse in downtown Minneapolis, Judge Nancy Brasel handed down just the 7th sentence in the sprawling Feeding Our Future fraud scandal. [The related juror bribery case has produced additional prison sentences.] KSTP-5 reports, Feeding Our Future defendant sentenced to over 1 year in prison Sentenced today was Abdul Abubakar Ali,...

The post Feeding Our Future: 7th sentence handed down appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] CEA Testimony Unravels Under Basic Questions at Finance Hearing
item.description:
A revealing moment at the March 27 Finance, Revenue and Bonding Committee hearing showed how quickly policy arguments can weaken when they are not grounded in the specifics of the […]

The post CEA Testimony Unravels Under Basic Questions at Finance Hearing  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] Evers Vetoes New Education Opportunities for Wisconsin Families
item.description:
WILL issued a statement in response to Governor Evers veto of Assembly Bill 602, legislation that would have had the effect of opting Wisconsin into a new federal tax credit scholarship program for K-12 students.

The post Evers Vetoes New Education Opportunities for Wisconsin Families appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Will Law] Evers Vetoes Bipartisan, Pro-Educator Legislation to Encourage Safe Classrooms
item.description:
WILL issued a statement in response to Governor Evers veto of AB 614 / SB 611, known as the Teacher Bill of Rights. The bill would have strengthened teachers’ authority to manage their classrooms, required parental notification when serious classroom incidents occur, and established clearer accountability so teachers can focus on teaching.

The post Evers Vetoes Bipartisan, Pro-Educator Legislation to Encourage Safe Classrooms appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] What Iowa Should Learn From Austin
item.description:
Voters in Austin, Texas overwhelmingly rejected a property tax increase, signaling frustration with rising costs and prompting the city to pursue an independent efficiency audit to improve accountability and control spending. Efficiency audits can identify waste, streamline operations, and generate meaningful savings—often improving services while reducing costs. Local governments in Iowa should undergo the same examination, reflecting the viewpoint of Governor Reynolds that real property tax relief requires structural reforms, transparency, and a willingness to evaluate how local governments operate.

The post What Iowa Should Learn From Austin appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Ending Taxpayer Support for Union Activity in Florida
item.description:
A new Freedom Foundation report documents how Florida's government unions have long benefited from tens-of-millions of dollars in taxpayer support. While recent legislation takes big strides towards ending union subsidies in the Sunshine State, more remains to be done.

The post Ending Taxpayer Support for Union Activity in Florida appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] YES on Constitutional Amendment 4: Begin to End the Inventory Tax
item.description:
The Pelican Institute recommends a YES vote on Constitutional Amendment 4. Sound tax policy should do two things well: raise revenue to fund essential services and create an environment where businesses can grow and invest. When taxes begin to penalize productivity, especially in ways that discourage investment, they hold back economic growth. That is the...

The post YES on Constitutional Amendment 4: Begin to End the Inventory Tax appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Pioneer Institute Supports the Ballot Initiative to Reduce the State Income Tax Rate from 5 to 4 Percent to Spur Jobs and Economic Growth
item.description:
At a legislative hearing today before the Special Committee on Initiative Petitions, Pioneer Institute Executive Director Jim Stergios testified on the urgency of addressing Massachusetts’ affordability crisis, slowing job growth, and continued loss of investment, and why a gradual reduction in the...

The post Pioneer Institute Supports the Ballot Initiative to Reduce the State Income Tax Rate from 5 to 4 Percent to Spur Jobs and Economic Growth  appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Louisiana Constitutional Amendment 5: Extend Judicial Retirement Age?
item.description:
The Pelican Institute is neutral on Constitutional Amendment 5, but it recommends voters weigh both possible short-term benefits with long-term challenges of our state’s sprawling judicial system. Constitutions often reflect a balance between consistency and adaptability. They establish rules meant to endure while leaving room for adjustment as circumstances change. Louisianans will soon consider Constitutional...

The post Louisiana Constitutional Amendment 5: Extend Judicial Retirement Age? appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Why Free-Market Capitalism Best Serves Kansas
item.description: Kansas does not need a new economic experiment. It needs more of what actually works. Free-market capitalism remains the best system ever discovered for human flourishing because it respects people as decision-makers, not as subjects of political management. It is built on private property, voluntary exchange, competition, and the rule of law. Those may sound […]

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] A Guide to Louisiana’s May 2026 Constitutional Amendments
item.description:
Constitutional amendments offer voters an opportunity to shape the fundamental rules of government. Some of this spring’s proposals will help determine how much power is centralized, how resources are allocated, and how easily policies can adapt to changing needs. If these amendments succeed, these reforms can promote accountability, reduce unnecessary constraints, and better align government...

The post A Guide to Louisiana’s May 2026 Constitutional Amendments appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Mackinac] Mackinac Center Applauds Bipartisan Action on Physician Licenses
item.description: It’s time for Michigan to pass a universal recognition law

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Citizens are fleeing bad policy - here's the proof
item.description: The growth of Idaho and Montana’s largest counties is not happening in isolation. It is part of a broader rebalancing across the West—one driven by policy, affordability, and opportunity.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] House Bill 670aas — Taxing districts; budget limits and new construction roll valuation (-5)
item.description: Bill Description: House Bill 670 [as amended in the Senate] has been completely rewritten to be a different bill and now amends existing law to revise provisions regarding certain limitations of budget requests by taxing districts and the value of new construction. Rating: -5 NOTE: House Bill 670 (originally unrated) was amended in the Senate […]

item.title: [Idahofreedom] House Bill 516a — K-12 instrn, sexual orientation (Teachers unions, no taxpayer funds) (+3)
item.description: Bill Description: House Bill 516 (H516) would prohibit school districts and public charter schools from using or authorizing the use of taxpayer funds to support teachers unions. Rating: +3 NOTE: H516 was initially written to remove an existing loophole in state statute permitting “age appropriate” instruction on sexual orientation or gender identity.” The legislation was […]

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Senate Bill 1336 — Civics education (+2)
item.description: Bill Description: Senate Bill 1336 (S1336) implements a number of instructional requirements for American history and government coursework in Idaho public schools and establishes new criteria for the selection of curricular materials. Rating: +2 Does it promote the breakdown of the traditional family or the deconstruction of societal norms? Examples include promoting or incentivizing degeneracy, […]

item.title: [Johnlocke] Columbia students seek to protest classroom activism
item.description:
Jessica Costescu and Jessica Schwalb write for the Washington Free Beacon about the latest questionable demands from one group of Ivy League students. Columbia University’s student union has been in a yearlong contract dispute with the Ivy League school, demanding everything from massive raises to dismantling ties to Israel. The union is also demanding an...

The post Columbia students seek to protest classroom activism appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Critiquing the latest ‘No Kings’ protests
item.description:
Chris Bray writes for the Federalist about the latest round of silly left-wing complaints. The American political left as a cultural formation, and the Democratic Party as an institution, have sunk into a style of oppositionalism that contains no opposition. They speak in a vernacular of protest because they speak in a vernacular of protest,...

The post Critiquing the latest ‘No Kings’ protests appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Johnlocke] Blair calls on left to end ‘unholy alliance’ with Islamists
item.description:
David Wilcock writes for the Daily Mail about interesting comments from one of Great Britain’s most successful Labour prime ministers. ‘Progressive’ politicians are failing to tackle Left-wing anti-Semitism because they won’t tackle the ‘unholy alliance’ between parts of their movement and hardline Islamists, Tony Blair has warned. The former prime minister said that figures on...

The post Blair calls on left to end ‘unholy alliance’ with Islamists appeared first on John Locke Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] Where did Michigan’s surplus go?
item.description: Heavy spending gets a paltry return

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Combating fare evasion could help lagging CTA ridership
item.description:
Fare evasion leads to lower revenue and higher crime. Two cities have found a way to fight it.

The post Combating fare evasion could help lagging CTA ridership appeared first on Illinois Policy.
